name: subagent-development
description: Execute plans using fresh subagent per task with code review between tasks. Use for high-quality iterative implementation.
capabilities: Provides step-by-step methodology for task delegation to subagents
Includes code review checkpoints between each task implementation
Defines quality gate criteria for pass/fail decisions
Offers templates for subagent prompts and reviewer prompts
Establishes critical rules for safe implementation workflows
author: DMJGilbert
securitySummary: This skill is pure documentation containing methodology guidelines for using subagents in development workflows. Static scanner flagged 27 pattern matches, but all are false positives - the scanner mistook markdown code blocks and development terminology for security risks. The skill contains no executable code, no network calls, no file system access, and no security risks.
valueStatement: Complex development plans often fail due to accumulated context errors and missing quality checks. This methodology ensures each task gets a fresh subagent with clean context, followed by mandatory code review before proceeding.
userTitle: Implement complex plans with fresh subagents and code reviews
limitations: Requires manual execution by the user - not automated
Depends on plan quality and task definition clarity
Adds overhead from frequent code reviews between tasks

useCases.title: Implement multi-component features
useCases.description: Break down complex features into isolated tasks, each implemented by fresh subagents with quality reviews

useCases.title: Onboard new developers safely
useCases.description: Use structured task delegation with built-in reviews to guide junior developers through complex implementations

useCases.title: Maintain code quality alone
useCases.description: Get external perspective through code reviewer subagents when working without human colleagues

promptTemplates.title: Basic Task Implementation
promptTemplates.scenario: First task in a feature implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement Task 1 from the plan: Create user authentication API. Follow TDD, write tests first, minimal code to pass. Output what was implemented and test results.

promptTemplates.title: Code Review Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my implementation of user authentication API. Check if it matches requirements, code quality, test coverage. Identify any critical issues.

promptTemplates.title: Final Integration Review
promptTemplates.scenario: All tasks completed, ready for merge
promptTemplates.prompt: Final review of complete user authentication feature. Verify plan compliance, architectural soundness, no regressions. Ready for production?

promptTemplates.title: Issue Resolution
promptTemplates.prompt: Fix the critical security issue identified in review: missing input validation. Update tests to cover edge cases. Re-review after fix.

bestPractices: Always use fresh subagents for each task to avoid context contamination
Never skip code reviews - they are your quality safety net
Document deferred issues instead of forgetting them
antiPatterns: Running multiple implementation subagents in parallel loses isolation benefits
Skipping reviews to save time creates technical debt
Implementing without referencing the plan causes drift from requirements

faq.question: Is this compatible with my existing development workflow?
faq.answer: Yes, it integrates with any workflow that uses task planning. You can adopt it gradually for complex features while keeping simple changes as-is.

faq.question: How many tasks should I break my plan into?
faq.answer: Aim for 2-4 hour tasks that produce working, testable code. Smaller tasks give better isolation but add review overhead.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: The methodology is manual but produces commit-ready code. Each task completion creates a logical commit point for your pipeline.

faq.question: Is my code safe during the review process?
faq.answer: Yes, reviews happen on completed code in your repository. The subagent only reads and comments, never modifies without permission.

faq.question: What if the reviewer finds too many issues?
faq.answer: Fix critical issues immediately, high priority before next task. Medium/low issues can be batched if they do not block progress.

faq.question: How does this compare to pair programming?
faq.answer: It provides similar quality benefits asynchronously. You get external perspective without scheduling coordination, though human pairs offer deeper context.
